$comment_schema: Cross-impl golden vectors for the Pages `Orphan` filter primitive (#1908 slice 2, increment b). Shape: { rows[], scenarios[] }. Each row is a page; scenarios apply an AND-composed list of FilterPrimitive wire objects and assert the SET of matching page ids. Only the matching-id set is compared cross-impl (representation-stable). Asserted by BOTH src-tauri/.../pages_orphan_conformance_tests.rs (real query path) and src/lib/tauri-mock/__tests__/orphan-conformance.test.ts (mock metaRowMatchesFilter).
$comment_scope: Covers the `Orphan` primitive — a page is an Orphan iff its materialised inbound_link_count is 0 AND it has NO outbound link to a block on a DIFFERENT page. Backend SQL (compile_orphan in src-tauri/src/filters/primitive.rs): `COALESCE(pc.inbound_link_count, 0) = 0 AND NOT EXISTS (… block_links where src.page_id = b.id AND tgt.page_id != b.id …)`. The mock evaluates `r.inboundLinkCount === 0 && !r.hasOutboundLink` (metaRowMatchesFilter in handlers.ts). Rows span the 2x2 of {inbound 0 / >0} x {hasOutboundLink true/false}. `Orphan` is paired against `HasNoInboundLinks` to show they DIFFER for a page with 0 inbound links but an outbound link (HasNoInboundLinks matches, Orphan does not).
$comment_seed: Each row carries { id, title, inboundLinkCount, hasOutboundLink }. On the Rust side `inboundLinkCount` is materialised into pages_cache (child_block_count 0); `hasOutboundLink==true` inserts a block_links edge whose source is the page itself and whose target is a SENTINEL page (id 01ORPHTARGET000000000000000, NOT assigned to the test space) so `tgt.page_id != b.id` fires and the page is excluded from Orphan but still lives outside the result space.

scenarios.name: Empty filter list — no constraint, every page passes
Orphan — inbound 0 AND no outbound link
HasNoInboundLinks — inbound 0 (looser than Orphan; includes the 0-inbound page with an outbound link)
AND-composition — Orphan AND HasNoInboundLinks (Orphan ⊂ HasNoInboundLinks, so equals Orphan alone)
